intent: Docstring-only update in src/oida_code/operator_soak/aggregate.py to align rule-5 wording with the QA/A35 sec.5.8-F tightening (rules 3 and 4 short-circuit before rule 5 fires, so reaching rule 5 implicitly requires false_positive_count < 2 AND false_negative_count < 2). No functional change. No test change. No schema change. Cherry-picked onto current main as `operator-soak/case-001-docstring-v2` so the audit-subject's tests/ stay in sync with main's models — preserving the 'single-commit docstring update' fixture intent.
notes:
Phase 5.8.1-C topology fix rerun on commit da9623a, target-ref operator-soak/case-001-docstring-v2 — supersedes the Phase 5.8.1-B useful_true_negative run.

History:
(1) run 24994865500 (27s, fail) — relative-path bug, fixed in commit 0b7a657.
(2) run 24995045522 (1m56s, success) — gateway-status=diagnostic_only, accepted=0, rejected=1 (silent rule-3 reject because pytest emitted no [E.tool.pytest.0] evidence). cgpro v1 labelled insufficient_fixture (UX 1/1/1/2).
(3) Phase 5.8.1 (commit da2bca1) — adapter patch: every error path emits a citable [E.tool.<binary>.0] diagnostic.
(4) Local verify-claims with synthetic post-patch packet revealed safety regression: 5.8.1 alone PROMOTED the false claim from rejected to accepted.
(5) Phase 5.8.1-B (commit 9b51def) — split diagnostic (citable, non-promoting) from actionable (citable, promoting) in the gateway loop. _enforce_requested_tool_evidence + _enforce_pass2_tool_citation key off actionable_evidence only.
(6) Recommendation regen (commit 2e19acf) — both enforcers now regenerate the report.recommendation string after demoting.
(7) run 25021820479 (1m45s, success) — gateway-status=diagnostic_only, accepted=0, unsupported=1, blocker='requested tool produced only diagnostic evidence'. cgpro labelled useful_true_negative (UX 2/2/1/2).
(8) Phase 5.8.1-C (commit da9623a) — verify-grounded --repo-root override + composite action plumbing. Bundle's tool_policy.repo_root='.' rebound at runtime to ${{ inputs.repo-path }} so pytest's cwd becomes the audit-subject directory.
(9) Cherry-picked 6585dd4 onto current main as new branch operator-soak/case-001-docstring-v2 (commit ddf302a). The original branch was cut before Phase 5.8-prep extended SOAK_STATUS_VALUES from 5 to 9 buckets and its tests/ would have failed on main's models even with a clean topology. Local pytest on the v2 branch: 52/52 passed.
(10) run 25022965745 (1m45s, success) — gateway-status=verification_candidate, accepted=1 (C.docstring.no_behavior_delta), rejected=0, unsupported=0. pytest status=ok with [E.tool.pytest.0] kind=test_result actionable evidence ('pytest passed scoped to ['tests/test_phase5_7_operator_soak.py'] with no failures'). cgpro labelled useful_true_positive (UX 2/2/2/2).

The Phase 5.8.1-C topology fix unblocked the verifier-grounded happy path. ADR-22 hard wall preserved through all 10 cycles; gateway-official-field-leak-count=0; forbidden-token scan over all run 25022965745 artefacts returned zero hits across (total_v_net, debt_final, corrupt_success, corrupt_success_ratio, verdict, merge_safe, merge-safe, production_safe, bug_free, verified, security_verified).

cgpro flagged 'External repo setup drift' as the next-cycle concern: case_002 (python-semver) and case_003 (markupsafe) need real audit packets AND target-specific test environments so dependency/build issues do not masquerade as verifier behaviour. The operator-soak.yml workflow currently installs only oida-code's dev deps in oida-main/, not the target repo's deps in oida-target/.
